idea.reasoning: Pinned idea for direct validation – Loadify.ai mobile QA automation
idea.description: Loadify – AI-powered mobile QA automation for outsourcing agencies. AI agents run test scenarios on real iOS/Android devices, auto-generate test cases from requirements, execute with pass/fail flags, and keep tests up-to-date when requirements change. Targets outsourcing companies (5-50 people) in Russia/CIS who do mobile app development for clients. Pricing: $1000/month SaaS or $2000-5000/month done-for-you testing service. Working alpha already exists. Value prop: fewer QA headcount, more bugs caught, scale client load without hiring.

landing_page.headline: Automate Mobile QA and Scale Without Hiring
landing_page.subheadline: For Russian/CIS mobile app outsourcing agencies who need to catch more bugs and handle more clients without adding QA staff
features: AI agents run test scenarios on real iOS/Android devices automatically
Auto-generate test cases from requirements in minutes, not days
Pass/fail flags let you see exactly what works and what doesn't
Tests auto-update when requirements change - no manual rework
Done-for-you service available from $2000/month if you want us to handle it all

persona.name: Dmitry Volkov
persona.current_workflow: Manual test case creation in Excel, execution on physical devices, bug tracking in Jira, regression testing takes 2+ weeks per release

top_struggles: Finding enough QA engineers for client projects
Clients constantly change requirements mid-sprint
Regression testing consumes 40% of team time
daily_life.daily_routine: Checks Jira for new bugs at 7:30AM, team standup at 9AM, reviews test cases until noon, client calls afternoons, automated reports evenings
daily_life.discovery_context: Sees LinkedIn ad during lunch break while researching automation tools

friction_points: $5000/month is 7x my discretionary budget - completely out of range
Even the $2000/month option is nearly 3x what I can afford
Recently regretted a purchase - making me very cautious about big commitments
Need to consult with my partner before such a large expense
Burned out mental state means I can't seriously evaluate this right now
simulations.reasoning: The product hits every single one of my pain points - finding QA engineers, handling requirement changes, and reducing the 40% regression testing time. But at $5000/month, this is simply not in my universe financially. I only have $700 discretionary, so even the $2000 'done-for-you' option is impossible. Given my recent purchase regret and cautious nature, I'd need at least 6 months of savings runway before considering this, and right now I'm just too burned out to pursue it.
simulations.competing_with: existing Jira/Slack workflow and manual QA processes

persona.name: Dmitry Kozlov
persona.current_workflow: Manual test case creation from requirements docs, execute on physical devices, track bugs in Jira, repeat for each client project
persona.current_tools: Jira, TestRail, BrowserStack, Firebase Crashlytics, Excel spreadsheets for test coverage

top_struggles: Clients constantly change requirements mid-sprint
Can't hire QA fast enough to match client load
Test coverage gaps causing bugs to reach production
daily_life.daily_routine: Wake early, check Slack for overnight client issues, review test results from offshore team, triage bugs, coordinate manual testing across 3 active projects, attend standups, manually update test cases when requirements change, late evening checking CI/CD pipelines
daily_life.discovery_context: Would find Loadify through LinkedIn ad or tech conference booth, likely during search for 'mobile QA automation' solutions

friction_points: $5000/month is 7x my entire discretionary budget and would completely drain my savings runway in a single month
I'm risk-averse and recently regretted a purchase - spending this much without proven ROI feels dangerous

My company budget for tools is likely much lower than $5000/month - this seems enterprise pricing
No clear case studies or ROI calculations specific to my agency size
simulations.reasoning: This product directly addresses my biggest pain points - requirement changes and test coverage gaps - and the CIS agency targeting is spot on. However, at $5000/month it's completely outside any realistic budget I could justify, especially with my cautious nature and recent purchase regrets. The $2000 option is more reasonable but still 3x my total tool budget. I'd need to consult my director and see enterprise pricing first, but honestly I doubt we'd allocate this much to a single tool when we can barely afford current subscriptions.
simulations.competing_with: BrowserStack and TestRail existing subscriptions ($320/month combined)
